task_completion.description: Configure the two satellites (Sat_Circular and Sat_Frozen) in GMAT using the Keplerian elements provided in the lunar_orbits.txt file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing Spacecraft configuration windows for Sat_Circular and Sat_Frozen, or a script editor showing their Keplerian elements being defined.

task_completion.description: Set up a Moon-centered coordinate system and configure the ForceModel to use the LP165P lunar gravity model at Degree 20 and Order 20.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Force Model configuration window showing the Central Body set to Luna/Moon, Gravity Field set to LP165P, and both Degree and Order set to 20.

task_completion.description: Set the propagation stopping conditions: Sat_Circular stops at Altitude <= 0 or 60 days elapsed; Sat_Frozen stops at exactly 60 days elapsed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Mission Sequence or Propagate command windows showing the stopping conditions for Sat_Circular (Altitude <= 0 or ElapsedDays = 60) and Sat_Frozen (ElapsedDays = 60).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/lunar_stability.script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal (e.g., 'ls ~/GMAT_output'), or text editor showing the file 'lunar_stability.script' saved in the 'GMAT_output' directory.

task_completion.description: Generate the analysis report at ~/GMAT_output/lunar_stability_report.txt containing the four required metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'lunar_stability_report.txt' with values for circular_lifetime_days, circular_min_altitude_km, frozen_lifetime_days, and frozen_min_altitude_km.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used GMAT to perform the simulation rather than hallucinating the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the GMAT GUI open with the mission configured, or terminal output showing GMAT executing the script.

integrity.description: Ensure the simulation was actually run to generate the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: GMAT message window showing run completion, orbit plots generated by GMAT, or GMAT report file outputs visible on screen.

integrity.description: Check that the report values match the expected physical simulation rather than being guessed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should show the circular orbit crashing (lifetime ~16 days, min altitude ~0 km) and the frozen orbit having a negative minimum altitude (~ -61 km) due to the provided parameters.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required script path is /home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_stability.script and the report path is /home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_stability_report.txt. The ForceModel must use the LP165P gravity model with Degree 20 and Order 20. Simulation facts: The circular orbit crashes within 16 days (lifetime ~16.0 days, min altitude <= 0 km). The frozen orbit parameters provided in the prompt actually place its initial periapsis below the lunar surface (altitude ~ -4.4 km, plunging to ~ -61.25 km during propagation). The agent is instructed to propagate it for exactly 60 days regardless, so the VLM should expect and accept negative altitudes for the frozen orbit in the final report.
